Pak Study MCQs · pages 701-750

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Which political party won Karachi by-election of NA-245 in August 2022?

Correct Answer: PTI
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan Tehreek-i-Insaf (PTI) won the by-election of Karachi’s NA-245 on 21 August 2022.

2

Mir Moazzam Minar is located in which city?

Correct Answer: Sukkur
Show explanation
Explanation

Mir Moazzam Minar is located in Sukkur, Sindh.



3

Sindh becomes first province to restore student unions after 38 years on____________?

Correct Answer: 11 February,2022
Show explanation
Explanation

Sindh becomes the first province to restore student unions after 38 years on 11 February 2022

****

Student unions had been banned across the country in 1984.

In an era of military dictator General Ziaul Haq.

The bill was presented by Sindh Minister for Parliamentary Affairs Mukesh Kumar Chawla.

****

The first student union in the United States was organized in 1832 at Harvard University.

In 1984, student unions were banned by the Ziaul Haq dictatorship.

ND27-8-2023

4

Dialing code of Hyderabad Pakistan

Correct Answer: 22
Show explanation
Explanation

enter 92 — the country code for Pakistan.

Then, dial 22 — the Hyderabad Pakistan area code.


5

The Mazar of famous ruler of Sindh Mian Yar Muhammad Kalhoro is in:

Correct Answer: Khudaabad

Explanation

Mian Yar Muhammad Kalhoro was pinier of actual rule of Kalhora clan in Sindh.

Mian Yar Muhammad Kalhoro had constructed Jamia Mosque Khudabad.

6

The city of perfume is called?

Correct Answer: Hyderabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Hyderabad is called the city of Perfumes

Hyderabad is known as The City of Pearls

Hyderabad is a city and the capital of the Hyderabad Division in the Sindh province of Pakistan.

It is the second-largest city in Sindh

and the eighth-largest in Pakistan

Founded: 1768

Then, dial 22 — the Hyderabad Pakistan area code.

The longest Airport in Pakistan is?

Correct Answer: Jinnah international airport
Show explanation

The owner/Operator of Quaid-e-Azam International Airport in Pakistan Civil Aviation Authority

Manager of Quaid-e-Azam International Airport: Afsar Malik

Location of Quaid-e-Azam International Airport Karachi, Sindh, Pakistan

Quaid-e-Azam International Airport opened in 1947 (76 years ago)

*******
Bacha Khan International Airport is in Peshawar.

Allama Iqbal International Airport is in Lahore.

Benazir Bhutto International Airport is in Islamabad.

Quaid-e-Azam International Airport is in Karachi.

Khokhrapar border is situated in which district--------?

Correct Answer: Tharparker
Show explanation
Explanation

Khokhrapar border is suited in the Tharparker district

Khokhropar is a border town situated in Tharparkar District, Sindh, Pakistan.

9

In which district of Sindh province Lakhi dar is situated?

Correct Answer: Shikarpur
Show explanation
Explanation

Shikarpur district, is a district in Sindh province in Pakistan.

The city of Shikarpur is the district headquarters.


10

How many new districts were created in 2004 in the Province of Sindh?

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation

In 2004, 4 new districts were formed - Qambar Shahdadkot district carved out of Larkana district; Jamshoro district out of the Dadu district; Kashmore district out of Jacobabad district and Umerkot district out of Tharparkar district

11

Which district was introduced in Karachi by the Sindh government in 2020?

Correct Answer: Kemari
Show explanation
Explanation

Kemari was introduced in August 2020.

**********

Total District in Pakistan 136

Facts about Pakistan Azla/Districts

1. How many districts are in Punjab?

36✔

And 9 Divisions

 

2. How many districts in KPK?

35 (after 25th amendment 1973- FATA merge)

And 8 Divisions

 

3. How many districts in Baluchistan?

35✔

And 7 Divisions

 

4. How many districts in Sindh?

30✔

And 7 Divisions


5. How many districts in Gilgit Baltistan?

14✔

 

6. How many districts are in Azad Kashmir?

10✔

 

7. The largest district of Pakistan by population?

Lahore✔

 

8. The smallest districts of Pakistan by area?

Tor Ghar✔ (KPK)

9. The largest districts of Pakistan by area?

Chagai✔(Balochistan)

  

10. The largest districts of Punjab by area?

Bhalwarpur✔

30✔

13

Karachi -----is the biggest district of Karachi in terms of population.

Correct Answer: Central
Show explanation
Explanation

Karachi Central is the most densely populated district not only among the six districts of Karachi

RankDistrictPopulation (2017 censusArea (Sq.Km.)Density
1Central2,971,3826943,063.51
2Korangi2,577,55610823,866.26
3East2,875,31513920,685.72
4South1,769,23012214,501.89
5West3,907,0659294,205.67
6Malir1,924,3642,160890.90
All100%16,024,8943,5274,543.49

Karachi is the most populous city in Pakistan

the 12th most populous city in the world, with a population of over 20 million

It is situated at the southern tip of the country

It is the former capital of Pakistan and the capital of the province of Sindh

There are seven districts of Karachi, namely:

  1. Karachi Central District
  2. Karachi East District
  3. Karachi South District
  4. Karachi West District
  5. Korangi District
  6. Malir District
  7. Keamari District

***

The largest district by area is Malir

**

Which of the following are not a date-producing area in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Mamsehra
Show explanation
Explanation

Most of the Dates produced in Pakistan are cultivated in Balochistan and Sindh provinces.

Major Dates producing areas in the country are:

  • Turbat
  • Panjgur
  • Gwadar
  • Khairpur
  • and D.I. Khan.

15

In which city is Quaid -e- Azam's tomb located?

Correct Answer: Karachi
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Quaid e Azam Mausoleum is located in Karachi.
  • Mazar-e-Quaid, also known as the National Mausoleum
  • Designed in the 1960s 
  • It was completed in 1971.
  • Architect: Yahya Merchant
  • It is an iconic symbol of Karachi

Additional information

  • Quaid-e-Azam Residency is situated in Ziarat.

16

Debul and Alor are the names of _______?

Correct Answer: Places of Ancient Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

Debul and Alor are the names of places of Ancient Sindh.

17

Which fruit is famous as "Sindhri" in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Mango
Show explanation
Explanation

The 'Sindhri' mango is a mango cultivar grown in Sindhri, a town in Sindh, and other areas of Sindh province in Pakistan. 

18

___________ desert in Mirpurkhas district is

Correct Answer: Nara Desert
Show explanation
Explanation

Nara Desert Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Mirpurkhas District, Sindh, Pakistan. The Nara desert provides refuge to much wildlife in the Sindh province.


-------
19

River Indus starts making delta near the city of ____?

Correct Answer: Thatta
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indus River starts in Tibet

The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

National River of Pakistan: Indus River

6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

Indus River Length: 3,180 km

Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh


20

What is the length of Ranikot fort?

Correct Answer: 32 kilometer, 20 (mi)
Show explanation
Explanation

Ranikot Fort is a Talpur fort near Sann, Jamshoro District, Sindh.

Ranikot Fort is also known as The Great Wall of Sindh.

The length of Ranikot Fort is 32 km

Ranikot Fort is believed to be the world's largest fort,

Ranikot fort's ramparts have been compared to the Great Wall of China.

Ranikot fort constructed during the administration of Nawab Wali Muhammed Leghari, the Prime Minister of Sindh during the Talpur dynasty

21

Peshawari chapel is traditional footwear of which province of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation

 North-West Frontier Province until 2010, when the name was changed to Khyber Pakhtunkhwa by the 18th Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan, and is known colloquially by various other names.

Which province of Pakistan is called “Land of Brave People”?

Correct Answer: KPK & GB
Show explanation
Explanation
23

Which province is chitral located in?

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation

Chitral is a town situated on the Chitral River in northern Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

Chitral served as the capital of the Chitral princely state that encompassed the region until its direct incorporation into West Pakistan in 1969. 

Area: 57 km²

Which Province to launch ‘Pothole Fixer App’ for reporting broken roads?

Correct Answer: KPK

Explanation

App Name Pothole Fixer App

It is an excellent and important step taken by the KP government to improve the infrastructure of the province.

As the majority of the roads especially in the rural areas have deteriorated and need quick fixing.

Which City name of Pakistan means,”The Forest in the Sky”??

Correct Answer: Shugran
Show explanation
Explanation

Shogran is a hill station situated on a plateau in the Kaghan Valley.

It is located in the province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

Shogran is located 34 kilometres away from Balakot.


31

Gateway of invaders is?

Correct Answer: Khyber Pass
Show explanation
Explanation

Traditional gateway for invaders, Khyber Pass is a 'sword that cut through the mountains'.

Khyber Pass was the route used by many invaders to conquer India, starting from Alexander the Great to the Mughals and the Afghans

32

When KP govt launched Rs25.9 billion food subsidy programme____?

Correct Answer: May 17, 2022
Show explanation
Explanation

Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government on May 17, 2022 announced Rs25.9 billion Insaf Food Card programme for five million residents.

 

33

When Khyber Pakhtunkhawa Assembly passed a bill to provide legal cover to Sehat Insaf Card scheme?

Correct Answer: June 3th 2022
Show explanation
Explanation

The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly passed the KP Universal Health Coverage Bill, 2022, to give legal cover to the provincial government`s flagship Sehat Insaf Card scheme, which covers 7.4 million families across the province with each family entitled to Rs1 million worth of free healthcare annually.

How many general seats Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has in the National Assembly?

Correct Answer: 45

Explanation

General Seats in National Assembly

Punjab: 141

Sindh: 61

Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: 45

Balochistan: 16

Islamabad: 3

Total general seats in NA: 266

35

World Bank approved $300 million for rural roads in _____ in June 2022.

Correct Answer: KPK

Explanation

Parashpura is the old name of which city?

38

where is Kalash valley?

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation

The valleys are surrounded by the Hindu Kush mountain range.

The inhabitants of the valley are the Kalash people, who have a unique culture, language and follow a form of ancient Hinduism. 

District: Chitral District

Province: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

Kamrat valley is situated in ?

Correct Answer: Upper Dir

Explanation

****

Kumrat Valley is situated at a distance of 370 km (230 miles) from the capital city of Islamabad 

Kumrat Valley is located about 45 minutes away from the town of Thal on the banks of the Panjkora River

Peshawar is the capital of the province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa,

43

Tear Lake also known as Ansoo Lake is located in which valley of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Kaghan Valley
Show explanation
Explanation

Ansoo Lake (which literally translates to teardrop lake)

Ansoo Lake is a tear-shaped lake

Ansoo Lake is located in Kaghan Valley in the Mansehra District of KPk the province of Pakistan.

Ansoo Lake is located at an elevation of 4,245 meters above sea level

Ansoo Lake is one of the highest lakes in the Himalayas Range.

The lake is said to have been discovered in 1993 by Pakistan Air Force 

ND18-4-2023

44

General seats in KPK assembly after merger of FATA are:

Correct Answer: 145
Show explanation
Explanation

After Final Delimitation 2018, the composition of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly seats is as under:

General seatsReserved for womenReserved for non-MuslimsTotal seats
115264145

After Merger of FATA in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in 2017.

Elections were held across the merged tribal districts on 20 July 2019.

285 candidates ran for elections on 16 general seats of tribal districts.

In addition to 16 general seats 4 seats will be reserved for women and 1 for Non-Muslim,

increasing the number of assembly seats from 124 to 145

ND18-5-2023

45

Which of the following passes connects Peshawar with Afghanistan?

Correct Answer: Khyber pass
Show explanation
Explanation

  • Important Passes of Pakistan

    1. Khyber Pass 

    connects: Peshawar to Kabul

    connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

    Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


    2. Bolan Pass 

    Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

    Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


    3. Lowari Pass 

    Connects: Chitral with Dir 

    Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


    4. Broghil Pass 

    Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

    Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


    5. Malakand Pass 

    Connects: Dir with Chitral 

    Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


    6. Shandur pass

    Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

    Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


    7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

    Other name: Friendship Gate

    Connects: China with Pakistan 

    Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


    8. Dorah pass

    Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

    Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


    9. Babusar Pass 

    Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


    10. Kohat Pass

    Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


    11. Khojak Pass

    Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


    12. Tochi Pass

    Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

 Important Passes of Pakistan

1. Khyber Pass 

connects: Peshawar to Kabul

connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

2. Bolan Pass

5. Malakand Pass

Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


6. Shandur pass

Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

Other name: Friendship Gate

Connects: China with Pakistan 

Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


8. Dorah pass

Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet

47

Ex-FATA comprises of _______?

Correct Answer: 7 Agencies
Show explanation
Explanation

The Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA) consisted of two types of areas:

1.      Tribal Agencies (Tribal Districts)

2.      Frontier Regions (FRs).

There were seven Tribal Agencies and six Frontier Regions
48

Khyber Pakhtunkhwa was first province to proclaim provincial educational autonomy in which year?

Correct Answer: 2011
Show explanation
Explanation

Khyber Pakhtunkhwa was the first province to proclaim provincial educational autonomy by passing the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Supervision of Curri­cula, Textbooks and Maintenance of Standards of Education Act, 2011.

49

Garam Chashma is located in which district which got international recognition by UNESCO?

Correct Answer: Chitral
Show explanation
Explanation

Two of the scenic tourist spots in KPK received international recognition as Biosphere Reserves by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (UNESCO).

One of the designated areas is Garam Chashma in Chitral, and the other extends from Bagnoter to Barian in Abbottabad district.

 

50

Which the city Military College of Engineering is located in?

Correct Answer: Risalpur
Show explanation
Explanation

The Military College of Engineering is an engineering college located at Risalpur in Nowshera District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

Founded: 1948

It was established by Pakistan Army

history:

A year after the Independence of Pakistan, the School of Military Engineering (SME) was established at Sialkot in 1948.

Having functioned at Sialkot for over four years, SME was shifted to Risalpur in 1952. 

 In 1958, the institution was renamed as Military College of Engineering (MCE).

Which district does muree fall in ?

Correct Answer: Rawalpindi
Show explanation
Explanation

Murree is a mountain resort town, located in the Galyat region of the Pir Panjal Range.

District Rawalpindi Tehsil Muree

It has average altitude of 2,291 metres

مری کو الگ ڈسٹرکٹ بنانے کے لیے کام ہورہا

59

Which desert stretches over the districts of Mianwali, Bhakkar, Khushab and D.G khan?

Correct Answer: Thal Desert
Show explanation
Explanation

The Thal desert is situated in Punjab, Pakistan. That extends between in Indus and Jhelum River near the Pothohar Plateau.

With a total length from north to south 190 miles,

The desert covers the districts of Bhakkar, Khushab, Mianwali, Layyah, Muzaffargarh as well as Jhang, from the left bank of the river Jhelum.

60

Punjab Public Library is located in?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

Govt. Punjab Public Library, Lahore, Pakistan, was established by order No. 2798 dated 8 November 1884 of the lieutenant governor of the Punjab, in the Home General Department.

It has been registered under the Societies Registration Act of 1860

Who built ‘Haran Minar’ _________?

Hiran Minar located: Sheikhupura

63

Which is most urbanized division of Punjab ?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

The divisions of Punjab  are the first-order administrative bodies of the Punjab Province of Pakistan.

There are 10 divisions,.

 Divisions are governed by Commissioners while districts are governed by Deputy Commissioners.

When by-elections held in 20 constituencies of Punjab?

Correct Answer: 17 July 2022

Explanation

By-elections on 20 Punjab Assembly seats which are left vacant by the de-seating of PTI dissidents held on 17 July 2022.

65

Quaid-e-Azam Solar Park Situated in which province ?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

Quaid-e-Azam Solar Park is located in the desert area of Lal Sohanra, Cholistan, Bahawalpur.

It is the largest solar plant in Pakistan.

Installed Capacity (MW) 400 / 600

66

In November 2022, Punjab became _____ province to introduce the third generation of the World Wide Web?

Correct Answer: First
Show explanation
Explanation

 The Punjab government on November 28, 2022 launched a Web 3.0 portal to become the first province in the country to introduce the third generation of the World Wide Web.

67

Which becomes first province to introduce the third generation of the World Wide Web?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

 The Punjab government on November 28, 2022 launched a Web 3.0 portal to become the first province in the country to introduce the third generation of the World Wide Web.

 

68

Changa Manga jungle is in which city?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

The Changa Manga is a planted forest and a wildlife park in Kasur District and Lahore District.

Established: 1866

the oldest hand-planted forest in the world.

the forest covers an area of 48.6 square kilometers (12,000 acres).

In Pakistan term, Changa Manga politics is used for Horse-trading

The name of the forest is derived from an amalgamation of the names of two brother dacoits (bandits), Changa and Manga

The Changa Manga forest can be entered from a road off the N-5 Highway 

***

Shakkarparian consists of a hill and a local park, it is situated in?

Correct Answer: Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Shakarparian is a hill, and a national park located near the Zero Point Interchange in Islamabad, Pakistan.

Shakarparian is a hill and local park about 609 meters above sea level.

Pakistan Monument and Pakistan Monument Museum are also located in Shakarparian.

Architect: Nasreddin Murat-Khan

  • List of 10 Divisions of Punjab
  • 1.       Bahawalpur
  • 2.       Dera Ghazi Khan
  • 3.       Faisalabad
  • 4.       Gujranwala
  • 5.       Gujrat
  • 6.       Lahore
  • 7.       Multan
  • 8.       Rawalpindi
  • 9.       Sahiwal
  • 10.   Sargodha
  • Gujrat given status of 10th division of Punjab on 17 August 2022.
  • Gujrat division consists of Gujrat, Mandi Bahauddin and Hafiz Abad districts.
72

The Upper Indus Plain consists of areas between Attock to:

Correct Answer: Mithankot
Show explanation
Explanation

Upper Indus plain height: 180m to 300m.

 Five rivers of Punjab drain this plain and cover it with alluvial soils.

73

The Pothohar Plateau forms the ______ of the Punjab?

Correct Answer: North
Show explanation
Explanation
The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

the total area of about 5000 miles2

located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River

300-600 meters is the height of the Pothohar Plateau
Pothohar Plateau is bounded on the east by the Jhelum River.
Pothohar Plateau is bounded on the west by the Indus River
74

The constituency of Punjab Assembly is called ________?

Correct Answer: PP
Show explanation
Explanation

A Member of the Provincial Assembly or MPA, is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district to the legislature or legislative assembly of a subnational jurisdiction.

75

Member of Provincial assembly is called________?

Correct Answer: MPA
Show explanation
Explanation

A Member of the Provincial Assembly or MPA, is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district to the legislature or legislative assembly of a subnational jurisdiction.

76

The constituency of Balochistan Assembly is called ________?

Correct Answer: PB
Show explanation
Explanation

The constituency of Balochistan Assembly is called PB.

Member of the assembly called MPA (Member of Provincial Assembly).

77

The constituency of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly is called ________?

Correct Answer: PK
Show explanation
Explanation

The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly is the unicameral legislative body of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province in Pakistan. It was established under Article 106 of the Constitution of the Pakistan. 

78

According to the constitution 1973, Local government is a subject of the ______.

Correct Answer: Provincial Governments
Show explanation
Explanation

The four provincial governments of Pakistan administer the four provinces of Pakistan.

The head of each province is a non-executive Governor appointed by the President.

 The Governors play a similar role, at the provincial level, as the President does at the federal level.


79

Who is the head of Tehsil?

Correct Answer: Tehsil Nazim
Show explanation
Explanation

 There are 154 districts in Pakistan including the Capital Territory and the districts of Azad Kashmir and Gilgit Baltistan. 

80

Sindh Occupies ____________ part of Pakistan.

Correct Answer: South Eastern
Show explanation
Explanation

Sindh is the third largest province of Pakistan by area and second largest province by population after Punjab.

Sindh is bordered by Balochistan province to the west and Punjab province to the north.

Sindh Capital: Karachi

81

The constituency of Sindh Assembly is called ________?

Correct Answer: PS
Show explanation
Explanation

Leader of the Opposition of Sindh Assembly

Haleem Adil Sheikh PTI since 26 January 2021

82

Pakistan exports first ever meat consignment to which country?

Correct Answer: Jordan
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan has exported its first-ever meat consignment to Jordan.

The development was announced by Advisor to Prime Minister for Commerce and Investment Abdul Razak Dawood.

The consignment was delivered by TATA Foods.

The companies are the Organic Meat Company Limited (TOMCL), Tata Best Food Limited, Tazji Meat and Food.

83

Bonded labour is mostly prevalent in :

Correct Answer: Kiln industry
Show explanation
Explanation

Donkeys, mules and horses work in traditional brick kilns - brick-making factories - in India, Pakistan, Nepal and Afghanistan.

Show explanation

It is the preeminent spiritual site of Sikhism.

Completed: 1589


97

Zakir Hussain was the______________ president of India.

Correct Answer: Third
Show explanation

Zakir Husain Khan served as the third president of India, from 13 May 1967 to 3 May 1969.

He previously served as the governor of Bihar from 1957 to 1962.

Second vice president of India from 1962 to 1967.

Correct Answer: Henry Vivian Derozio

when Abu ala Maududi was awarded death punishment by military court on” Qadiani issue”_____?

What was the title given to Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan? Mohsin-e-Pakistan

Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan died on 10 October 2021, at the age of 85 after being transferred to a hospital in Islamabad with lung problems.

Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan (TTP) was a political party in Pakistan founded and led by nuclear scientist Abdul Qadeer Khan.  In September 2013, the party was dissolved by Abdul Qadeer Khan.

who travelled extensively in the lands of Afro-Eurasia, largely in the lands of Dar al-Islam, travelling more than any other explorer in pre-modern history, totalling around 117,000 km, surpassing Zheng

He with about 50,000 km and Marco Polo with 24,000 km.

She was elected to the Punjab Assembly on a reserved seat for women in 1985 and then the 1990 elections.

She served as an MNA on a seat reserved for women in 2002 and 2008.

Born: June 4, 1857

Died: November 18, 1914

114

When Muhammad bin Qasim conquered Dibal (khi)?

Correct Answer: 712 AD
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Facts about Muhammad bin Qasim
  • Nirun, fortress (called Sikka), Brahmanabad, Alor, Multan and Gujrat
  • close relative of Hajjaj bin Yousuf
  • Governor of Persia
  • The Umayyad caliphate ordered Muhammad Bin Qasim to attack Sindh
  • At Rohri he was met by Dahir’s forces. Dahir died in the battle, his forces were defeated
  • After the conquest of Multan, he carried his arms to the borders of the Kingdom of Kashmir, but his dismissal stopped the further advance.
  • death on 18th of July, 715AD at the age of twenty

Former Interior Minister Rehman Malik passed away on:

Correct Answer: February 23, 2022

Rehman Malik died at the age of 70 on 23 February 2023

Rehman Malik belonged to Pakistan Peoples Party.

Rehman Malik served as the interior minister of Pakistan from 2008 to 2013.

He served as the interior minister of Pakistan from 2008 to 2013.

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan is buried in:

Correct Answer: Aligarh UP India
Show explanation
Explanation

Sir Syed Ahmad Khan died on March 27, 1898.

He is buried right along with the Sir Syed Masjid inside Aligarh University, Aligarh UP India.

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was born in 1817 in Delhi


Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was an Islamic reformer and philosopher

Sir Syed Ahmad Khan received his early education from his Maternal Grand Father



118

When Sir Syed Ahmed Khan became member of Imperial committee?

Correct Answer: 1878
Show explanation
Explanation

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan became a member of the Imperial Legislative Council in 1878,

Serving from July 1878 to July 1880 as an additional member.

He also served a second term that lasted until 1883.

******

  • Got Knowledge From Farid Ud Din (Maternal-grandfather – Ex Mughal Pm)
  • Joined Gov’t In 1839  => Clerical Job naib munshi (administrative assistant)
  • Promoted As Sub-judge 1841
  • Transferred To Delhi  Chief Judge In 1846 
  • Member Of Imperial Council 1886



  • Aligarh Movement Objective:
  • 1. Modern Education For Muslims To Compete Hindus 
  • 2. Cooperation With The British Government


  • Established:

  • 1. Schools   • Muradabad (1859)  • Ghazipur (1863)
  • 2. Scientific Society At Gahazipur (1864)
  • 3. Muhammadan Educational Conference 1866

  • 4. Muhammadan Anglo-oriental College =>
  • Mao High School was Established in 1875
  • Got Status Of College 1877  
  • Status Of University 1920

Punjab Chief Minister Sardar Usman Buzdar _____________ replaced Fayyaz-ul-Hassan Chohan with Dr Firdous Aashiq Awan as special assistant to chief minister on information.

Correct Answer: 2 November 2020
Show explanation
Explanation

This is for the second time that portfolio of Fayyaz Chohan has been changed. He was first removed as Information Minister in 2019.

Mr Chohan was holding additional charge of information department but he has been allowed to retain the portfolio of Colonies department. Dr Firdous Awan has been appointed as Special Assistant to the Chief Minister on Information in place of Javaid Akhtar.

Updated November 5, 2020

Usmani, he was a Pakistani nuclear physicist and an official who later chaired the Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission (PAEC). He also oversaw the establishment of the Space Exploration Commission until 1971.

When Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto became foreign minister of Pakistan?

first term of FM:  15 June 1963 – 31 August 1966 

Appointed by President: Muhammad Ayub Khan

Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto Preceded by: Muhammad Ali Bogra

**

Second term Of FM: 20 December 1971 – 28 March 1977

Appointed by President: Fazal Ilahi Chaudhry

Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto Preceded by: Yahya Khan

Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto History 

Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was the president of Pakistan from 20 December 1971 - 14 August 1973

the first term: fourth prime minister of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973

the second term: ninth prime minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977

He was the founder of the Pakistan People's Party

Nickname: Quaid-e-Awam ("the People's Leader")

Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was Born on January 5, 1928, in Larkana

Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was hanged to death on 4 April 1979 District Jail Rawalpindi

Resting place: Bhutto family mausoleum

Children:

Benazir Bhutto

Sanam Bhutto

Murtaza Bhutto

Shahnawaz Bhutto

**

7th Speaker of the National Assembly from 14 April 1972 – 15 August 1972

Minister of Commerce from 28 October 1958 – 16 January 1960

ND15-10-2023

The late Dr Ruth Pfau came Pakistan from Germany in the year_____?

Correct Answer: 1960
Show explanation
Explanation

Ruth Pfau was a German–Pakistani Catholic nun of the Society of the Daughters of the Heart of Mary, and a physician.

Ruth Pfau moved from Germany to Pakistan in 1961.

Ruth Pfau was honoured with the Hilal-i-Pakistan-, Hilal-i-Imtiaz-, Nishan-i-Quaid-i-Azam-, and the Sitara-i-Quaid-i-Azam award for her services to Pakistan.

Dr Pfau, who was hailed as the Mother Teresa of Pakistan, passed away on 10 August 2017 in Karachi.

  • Joined Gov’t In 1839  => Clerical Job naib munshi (administrative assistant)

  • Aligarh Movement Objective:
  • 1. Modern Education For Muslims To Compete Hindus

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was the founder of the -----movement.

Syed Ahmed Khan started the Aligarh movement for the educational upliftment of the Muslim community.

important question related to *Sir Syed Ahmad Khan*

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was an Islamic pragmatist, Islamic reformer, and philosopher.

He was born on October 17, 1817, in Delhi

Sir Syed Ahmad Khan died on March 27, 1898. He is buried right along with Sir Syed Masjid inside Aligarh University, Aligarh UP India.

Sir Syed Ahmad Khan received his early education from his Maternal Grand Father

Two nation theory was first time introduced by sir Syed

***

ND29-11-2022

200

Allan Faqir was a Sufi Singer of which language?

Correct Answer: Sindhi
Show explanation
Explanation

Sindhi Sufi/folk singer Allan Fakir was awarded with President's Pride of Performance Award in 1980 for his unique singing.

Allan Fakir died on 4 July 2000 , buried near Sindh University Jamshoro.

201

Which Pakistani Actor will be starring in the Netflix series ‘The Crown’?

Correct Answer: Humayun Saeed
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistani star Humayun Saeed is set to feature as Dr. Hasnat Khan, who was in a relationship with Princess Diana, on the upcoming fifth season of the acclaimed Netflix series “The Crown”.


202

Indian Musalmans is written by ___

Correct Answer: William Wilson Hunter
Show explanation
Explanation

Sir William Wilson Hunter  was a Scottish historian, statistician, a compiler and a member of the Indian Civil Service.

He is most known for The Imperial Gazetteer of India on which he started working in 1869, and which was eventually published in nine volumes in 1881

203

The author of the book "Izalat-al-Akhifa" is ___

Correct Answer: Shah Waliullah
Show explanation
Explanation

Shah Waliullah Dehlawi, was an Islamic scholar, muhaddith reformer.

Historiographer bibliographer Theologian and philosopher.

He joined local forces and defeated Marathas at battle of Panipat in 1761.


204

The book "Tabqat e Akbari" was written by ___

Correct Answer: Khwaja Nizam-ud-Din Ahmad
Show explanation
Explanation

Khwaja Nizam-ud-Din Ahmad was a Muslim historian of late medieval India.

He was son of Muhammad Muqim-i-Harawi.

He was Akbar's Mir Bakhshi.


205

"The Quaid-i-Azam during his last day" is written by?

Correct Answer: Ilahi Bakhsh
Show explanation
Explanation

This is a first-hand account of the last days of the founder of Pakistan, the Quaid-i-Azam Mohammad Ali Jinnah written by Lieutenant Colonel Ilahi Bakhsh, a doctor of medicine,

Lieutenant Colonel Ilahi Bakhsh was attending to Mohammad Ali Jinnah during his last illness.

206

Urdu translation of Shah latif Jo Risalo , was made by?

Correct Answer: Shaikh ayaz
Show explanation
Explanation

Shah Jo Risalo is a poetic compendium of famous Sindhi Sufi poet Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai.

207

Sindhu Muhinje Saah Mein” Famous book of Sindhi literature , written by__________?

Correct Answer: Amar Jaleel
Show explanation
Explanation

Kazi Abdul Jaleel, popularly known as Amar Jaleel, is a Sindhi fiction writer and a Columnist whose columns appear in various Sindhi, Urdu and English-language dailies of Pakistan.

208

The first published urdu book is _______?

Correct Answer: Dan Majlis
Show explanation
Explanation

Urdu novel, Nazir Ahmad Dehlvi published Mirat-ul-Uroos, the first original novel in Urdu in 1869.

209

“Qissa Sohni Mahinwal” was written by____________?

Correct Answer: Fazal Shah
Show explanation
Explanation

Fazal Shah Sayyad was a Punjabi poet known for his qissas on tragic romances such as Sohni Mahiwal, Heer Ranjha and Laila Majnu.

210

Who is the writer of toward Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Ch Waheed-uz-Zaman
Show explanation
Explanation

Ch Waheed-uz-Zaman is the writer of toward Pakistan. 

211

Which of the following is written by Maulana Abul Kalam Azad

Correct Answer: Tazkirah
Show explanation
Explanation

Maulana  Abul Kalam Azad was an Indian scholar and the senior Muslim leader of the Indian National Congress during the Indian independence movement.


212

Which of the following is a journal written by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan

Correct Answer: Tehzeeb-ul-Akhlaq
Show explanation
Explanation

Tehzeeb-ul-Ikhlaq was a journal published by the Muslim reformer Sir Syed Ahmed Khan between 1871 and 1897.

The journal published alternative Muslim perspectives, written in plain language.

It gave voice to the publisher's religious, social, and reforming opinions

213

Ba salamat Ravi written by?

Correct Answer: Colonel Mohammad Khan
Show explanation
Explanation

محمد خان 5 اگست 1910ء کو ضلع چکوال کے ایک گاؤں بلکسر میںپیدا ہوئے۔ ابتدائی تعلیم گاؤں میں حاصل کی ۔1931ء میں اسلامیہ کالج لاہور سے بی اے کیا۔ پھر 1934ء میں ایم اے اقتصادیات اور 1935ء میں بی ٹی کا امتحان پاس کیا۔

پہلی بار گاؤں کے سکول میں بلی چوہے کی کہانی لکھی جو بچوں کے رسالے ’’رہنمائ تعلیم‘‘ میں شائع ہوئی۔ پہلی کتاب ’’بجنگ آمد ‘‘ نے بہت شہرت پائی ۔ اس کے علاوہ ان کی دو کتابیں ’’بسلامت روی ‘‘ اور ’’بزم آرائیاں‘‘ ہیں۔

Correct Answer: Laurence Frederic Rushbrook Williams

235

Which of the following Allama Iqbal Persian Books?

Correct Answer: All of these
Show explanation
Explanation

List of Allama Iqbal Persian Books

1.    the Asrar-i-Khudi اسرارِ خودی (Secrets of the Self)

2.    Rumuz-i-Bekhudi رموزِ بیخودی (Hints of Selflessness)

3.    the Payam-e-Mashriq پیامِ مشرق (The Message of the East)

4.    The Zabur-e-Ajam زبورِ عجم (Persian Psalms), published in 1927,

5.    the Javed Nama جاوید نامہ (Book of Javed), published in 19232

6.    Pas Chih Bayed Kard Ay Aqwam-e-Sharq پس چہ باید کرد اے اقوامِ شرق

236

Which of the following Allama Iqbal English Books?

Correct Answer: Both of these
Show explanation
Explanation

List of Allama Iqbal English books

 

1.    The Development of Metaphysics in Persia

2.    The Reconstruction of Religious Thought in Islam,

237

Which book was published after the death of Allama Iqbal?

Correct Answer: Aramaghan-E-Hijaz
Show explanation
Explanation

 Aramaghan-E-Hijaz was after a few month of Allama Iqbal`s death.

238

Which book of Allama Iqbal was published on Economy?

Correct Answer: Ilm-Al-Iqtisad
Show explanation
Explanation

 Ilm-Al-Iqtisad book was published in 1903.

239

Which book does not belong to Allama Iqbal?

Correct Answer: Hayat e Javid
Show explanation
Explanation

 Hayat e Javid book written by Altaf Hussain Hali

the First Book of Allama Iqbal Ilm ul Iqtisad

Allama Iqbal married three times

1.       Karim Bibi 1895

2.       Mukhtar Begum, 1914-1924

3.       Sardar Begum, (1924-2015)

 

List of Allama Iqbal all Books.

1- Allama Iqbal Persian Books

1.    the Asrar-i-Khudi اسرارِ خودی (Secrets of the Self)

2.    Rumuz-i-Bekhudi رموزِ بیخودی (Hints of Selflessness)

3.    the Payam-e-Mashriq پیامِ مشرق (The Message of the East)

4.    The Zabur-e-Ajam زبورِ عجم (Persian Psalms), published in 1927,

5.    the Javed Nama جاوید نامہ (Book of Javed), published in 19232

6.    Pas Chih Bayed Kard Ay Aqwam-e-Sharq پس چہ باید کرد اے اقوامِ شرق

 

2. Allama Iqbal Urdu books

 

1.    Bang-e-Dara بانگِ درا (The Call of the Marching Bell) First in Urdu, 1924

2.    Zarb-i-Kalim ضربِ کلیم (or The Rod of Moses)

3.    Armughan-e-Hijaz ارمغانِ حجاز

 

3. Allama Iqbal English books

 

1.    The Development of Metaphysics in Persia

2.    The Reconstruction of Religious Thought in Islam,

 

4. Allama Iqbal Punjabi Books/Poem

Iqbal also wrote some poems in Punjabi, such as "Piyaara Jedi" and "Baba Bakri Wala"

Which book(s) belongs to Allama Iqbal?

Correct Answer: All of these
Show explanation
Explanation

The first collection of poetry, written in Persian, was published in 1915, with the title Israr e khudi

the First Book of Allama Iqbal Ilm ul Iqtisad

1- Allama Iqbal Persian Books

1.    the Asrar-i-Khudi اسرارِ خودی (Secrets of the Self)

2.    Rumuz-i-Bekhudi رموزِ بیخودی (Hints of Selflessness)

3.    the Payam-e-Mashriq پیامِ مشرق (The Message of the East)

4.    The Zabur-e-Ajam زبورِ عجم (Persian Psalms), published in 1927,

5.    the Javed Nama جاوید نامہ (Book of Javed), published in 19232

6.    Pas Chih Bayed Kard Ay Aqwam-e-Sharq پس چہ باید کرد اے اقوامِ شرق

 

2. Allama Iqbal Urdu books

 

1.    Bang-e-Dara بانگِ درا (The Call of the Marching Bell) First in Urdu, 1924

2.    Zarb-i-Kalim ضربِ کلیم (or The Rod of Moses)

3.    Armughan-e-Hijaz ارمغانِ حجاز

249

Who is called the Shakespeare of Punjabi literature?

Correct Answer: Waris Shah
Show explanation
Explanation

Waris Shah called the Shakespeare of Punjabi literature.

Agha Hashar Kashmiri is the Shakespeare of Urdu literature.

250

Pervez Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire published in the year_?

Correct Answer: 2006
Show explanation
Explanation

Know everything about Musharraf

Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

Died: 5 February 2023

Dying place: Dubai, UAE

Disease: amyloidosis

Aged: 79

Buried: Karachi

 

 

251

The other name of Babar Nama is

Correct Answer: Tazkara e Babri
Show explanation
Explanation

The Bāburnāma alternatively known as Tuzk-e Babri is the memoirs of Ẓahīr-ud-Dīn Muhammad Bābur .

Founder of the Mughal Empire and a great-great-great-grandson of Timur.

Uchau, a festival is celebrated in_____________?

A scrambling and trailing vine-like plant, it was originally domesticated in Africa.

263

264

Which of these is not usually a meat dish ?

Explanation

from Kalabagh to Makarwal over a stretch length of 75 KM in District Mianwali.

266

Tethys’s deposits finally turned to be___________?

Correct Answer: Himalayas
Show explanation
Explanation

Tethyan deposits can be found in North America and Eurasia (especially in the Alpine and Himalayan regions) and in southern Asia 

267

Valley of Sakesar a beautiful Valley, located in which district of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Khushab
Show explanation
Explanation

The Valley of Sakesar lies on the outer fringes of the Soon Valley in the Khushab District, Punjab.

The Valley of Sakesar's height is 1,522m.

The district consists of four tehsils:

  1. Khushab
  2. Noorpur Thal
  3. Quaidabad
  4. Naushera




268

Natural uranium is found in:

Correct Answer: NWFP
Show explanation
Explanation

NWFP

Uranium is also found in small amounts in most rocks and  seawater. Uranium mines operate in many countries, but more than 85% of uranium is produced in six countries: Kazakhstan, Canada, Australia, Namibia, Niger and Russia.

Which of the following is rich in Aluminum?

Uranium deposits are found in which city of Punjab___?

sulfur deposits are found in the district of?

273

________ has been discovered at Kalabagh.

274

Chromite deposits are found in Pakistan in the area of___________?

Correct Answer: None of these
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Chromite from Zhob Valley, Quetta District, Balochistan Region, Pakistan.
  • In Pakistan, Chromite was first discovered by Vredenburg (1901).
  •  the subsequent mining began in 1903 in Khanozai, Zhob district.
  • By 1915, the mining stretched to the Sra-Salawat area, 29 km south of Muslimbagh.
  • At present, chromite is mined in Baluchistan and Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa
  • ****
  • Balochistan has the most mineral deposits among the provinces of Pakistan, with Sindh rich in coal deposits and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa rich in gems. 
275

Reko Diq reserves of Balochistan discovered in the year of:

Correct Answer: 1978
Show explanation
Explanation

Geological Survey of Pakistan had discovered the Reko Diq reserves way back in 1978.

Reko Diq is located in Chaghi District of Balochistan.

 

276

Which of the following has world's second largest Salt Mines?

Correct Answer: Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

The Khewra Salt Mine is located in Khewra, north Jhelum District, Punjab, Pakistan, which rises from the Indo-Gangetic Plain.

It is Pakistan’s largest and oldest salt mine and the world’s second-largest mine.

Khewra Salt Mine another name is Mayo Salt Mine

277

Which area of Pakistan has the biggest deposit of copper?

Correct Answer: Chaghi
Show explanation
Explanation

Reko Diq represents one of the largest copper and gold reserves in the world 

The Reko Diq Mine is a planned mining operation, located near Reko Diq town in Chagai District

The biggest deposit of copper is present in Reko Diq, discovered in 1969.

Tethyan is seeking to develop the Reko Diq copper-gold deposit in the Chagai Hills

Reko Diq is a town in Balochistan.

278

Thermal electricity is produced from.?

Correct Answer: Fossil fuel

Explanation

Thermal energy sources are fossil fuels like:

natural gas, coal, and oil

as well as solar heat

heat pump electric heat

and geothermal heat.

279

Where will you find Hamun-i-MashKel Lake?

Correct Answer: In Balochistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Hamun e-Mashkel is the largest seasonal desert lake in Pakistan, is located in Washuk district of Balochistan.

Hamun e-Mashkel is approximately 85 km long and 35 km wide.

280

Sindh Sagar is between the rivers of:

Correct Answer: Indus and Jhelum
Show explanation
Explanation

Doab, a Persian term, signifies an area between two rivers. 

281

Most of Pakistan`s rivers flow into:

Correct Answer: Indus
Show explanation
Explanation

Indus is the longest river of Pakistan.

282

Doaaba Chaj is located between River Chenab and River ____.

Correct Answer: Jhelum
Show explanation
Explanation

Doab Chaj lies between the Chenab and Jhelum.

Doab Chaj lies on the southern fringes of the Kashmir valley.

Chaj Doab can be classified as one of the main regions of Punjab, Pakistan

This doab covers:

 Gujrat District

Bhimber District

Mirpur District

Mandi Bahauddin District

Sargodha District

Lalian Tehsil of Chiniot District 

some areas of Jhang Tehsil of Jhang District.

***

Five Important Doabs of Pakistan 

Sindh Sagar Doab is b/w: Indus & Jhelum rivers 

Jech Doab is b/w: Jhelum & Chenab rivers 

Bari Doab is b/w: Ravi & Sutlej rivers

Rachna Doab  is b/w: Ravi  & Chenab rivers  

Bias Doab is b/w: Bias & Sutlej rivers 

Doab Chaj is b/w: Chenab and Jhelum.

283

Which of the following lake in Gilgit Baltistan was created as a result of Natural disaster in 2010?

Correct Answer: Attaabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Attabad Lake is a lake located in the Gojal region of Hunza Valley in Gilgit−Baltistan, Pakistan.

It was created in January 2010 as the result of a major landslide in Attabad.

284

Hussaini Suspension bridge is built on which river ?

Correct Answer: Hunza
Show explanation
Explanation

Hunza is a mountainous valley in the Gilgit-Baltistan.

Hunza is situated at the bank of Hunza river .

The Hunza valley is popular for its snow-capped and rough mountains.




285

River Jhelum rises in the Himalayas and meets the River Chenab in southwest of Jhang. Its length is ____ kms.

Correct Answer: 725
Show explanation
Explanation

Jhelum River, river of northwestern India and northern and eastern Pakistan.

It constitutes the westernmost of the five rivers of the Punjab region that merge with the Indus River in eastern Pakistan.

Chenab River rises in Himalayas and flows into Pakistan. Its length is ____ kms.

Correct Answer: 1,087
Show explanation

287

Name the Pakistan’s Commissioner for Indus Waters?

Correct Answer: Mehr Ali Shah
Show explanation
Explanation

The 117th meeting of the India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) held in March, 2022

This meeting takes place on yearly basis.

The Indian delegation comprising 10 members was headed by the Indian Commissioner for Indus Waters PK Saxena, while Pakistan’s delegation was led by Pakistan Commissioner for Indus Waters Syed Muhammad Mehar Ali Shah.

 

288

Which river passes through Chitral?

Correct Answer: Kunar
Show explanation
Explanation

Chitral is a town situated on the Chitral River in northern Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

Chitral served as the capital of the Chitral princely state that encompassed the region until its direct incorporation into West Pakistan in 1969. 

Area: 57 km²

Drigh Lake is in district?

290

Indus water commission meeting 2021 was held in ------

Correct Answer: New delhi
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) is a water-distribution treaty between India and Pakistan.
  • Indus Waters Treaty brokered by the World Bank
  • It was signed in Karachi on 19 September 1960
  • Sighed by Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan.
  • the following 3 rivers were given to Pakistan: Indus, Jhelum and Chenab  
  • the following 3 rivers were given to India: Sutlej, Beas, Ravi.
291

Which river is located in district Pakpattan?

Correct Answer: Sutlej

293

Which of the following pass connects Dera Ismail Khan with Ghazni (Afghanistan)?

Correct Answer: Gomal pass
Show explanation
Explanation

Gomal Pass connects Ghazni in Afghanistan with Tank and Dera Ismail Khan in Pakistan.

The Gumal Pass is actually a 4-mile (6-km) defile (gorge), but the name is sometimes applied to the full course of the Gumal River.


294

Anchar Lake is found in:

Correct Answer: Jammu and Kashmir India
Show explanation
Explanation

Anchar Lake is a lake located near Soura area in the city of Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir, India.

Situated close to Ganderbal, the lake is connected with the famous Dal Lake via a channel "Amir Khan Nallah" which passes through Gilsar and Khushal Sar. The lake is in a highly deteriorated condition.

295

AL Tarbela dam, pressurized water runs through ?

Correct Answer: gases pass through narrow opening
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The blades spin at high speed and compress or squeeze the air.
  • The compressed air is then sprayed with fuel and an electric spark lights the mixture.
  • The burning gases expand and blast out through the nozzle, at the back of the engine.
  • As the jets of gas shoot backward, the engine and the aircraft are thrust forward

The largest lake in Jammu and Kashmir?

all rivers of Punjab enter into the Indus River?

Correct Answer: kot Mithan
Show explanation
Explanation

***

  • Indus river end at the Arabian sea
  • The Indus River rises near Mansarovar in Tibet and flows through India, Pakistan, and finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.
  • ***
  • The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

  • Indus River Length: 3,180 km

    Indus River Mouths: the Arabian Sea, Rann of Kutch, Nalsarovar bird sanctuary

    Indus River Tributaries: Sutlej River, Chenab River, Jhelum River, Ravi River,

    Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

how many rivers flow in Punjab Province?

5

How many rivers flow in Sindh Province?

4

How many rivers flow in Balochistan Province?

7

How many rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province?

8

How many rivers are present in Pakistan?

304

Gomal and Kuram rivers are in the province of:

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Gomal and Kuram rivers are in the province of KPK
  • ***
  • Kabul, Swat, Kunhar, Panjkora, Bara, Kurram, and Gomal are the rivers of KPK
  • Khyber Pakhtunkhwa often abbreviated as KPK and formerly known as the North-West Frontier Province, is one of the four provinces of Pakistan.

  • ****
  • Gomal River is a 400-kilometer-long (250 mi) river in Afghanistan and Pakistan
  • Gomal River rises in the northern part of Afghanistan's Paktika Province. It joins the Indus River 20 miles south of Dera Ismail Khan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
305

The four rivers that makes Panjnad are _____?

Correct Answer: Ravi, Sutlej, Jhelum and Chenab
Show explanation
Explanation

What is Panjnad?

Panjnad is the name of a river comprises with four rivers Ravi, Sutlej, Jhelum and Chenab

 

The length of Panjnad is 74 Kilometers

 

Panjnad falls in River Indus

 

Panjnad comprised of how many rivers?

Four (4)

 

The four rivers that makes Panjnad are

Ravi, Sutlej, Jhelum and Chenab

 

306

Kachhi Canal Project start from ______?

Correct Answer: Taunsa Barrage
Show explanation
Explanation

The Kachhi Canal Project is a 499-km long canal project situated in the Balochistan and Punjab.

It starts from Taunsa Barrage at Indus River and terminates in Balochistan.

The project was started in 2002, was almost abandoned due to grossly cost 

The length of main canal is 399 km, out of which 305 km lies in Punjab region while 94 km are in Balochistan.

Total System Length after completion of all (three) phased will be 499 KM [399KM + 60KM+ 40 KM], Baluchistan would get 194 KM, and Punjab will get 304 KM of Kanal.



RK03-12-2022

307

The sheosar lake is located at......?

Correct Answer: Gilgit Baltistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Sheosar Lake means 'Blind Lake' in the Shina language, as Sheo means 'Blind' while Sar means 'Lake'

Location Deosai Plains, Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan (Karakoram-West Tibetan Plateau alpine steppe)

The lake is accessible through two routes. One is via Astore and the other via Skardu.

Max. length         2.3 kilometers (1.4 mi)

Max. width         1.8 kilometers (1.1 mi)

Average depth 40 meters (130 ft)

Surface elevation   4,142 meters (13,589)

** 

308

Inflation in Pakistan rises to ________ % in October 2021.

Correct Answer: 9.2%
Show explanation
Explanation

The annual inflation rate in Pakistan edged up to 9.2 percent in October of 2021 from 9.0 percent in the previous month.

 increased to 24.6% on a year-on-year basis in Oct 2022 

Pakistan's inflation rate accelerated to 38% in May of 2023 from 36.4% in April.

This was the highest reading since comparable records began in 1957, mainly due to rising food inflation (48.7% vs 48.1% in April).

Paristan Lake is in district?

It is the highest lake in Pakistan

and also one of the highest lakes in the world.

311

Jinnah Barrage has been built on River?

Correct Answer: Indus
Show explanation
Explanation

The Jinnah Barrage is a barrage on the River Indus near Kalabagh, Pakistan.

It is part of the Thal Project which helps irrigate 770,000 ha in the Sindh Sagar Doab east of the Indus

Jinnah Barrage is in the district Mianwali of Punjab near Kalabagh 

Jinnah Barrage construction began in 1939 and opened in 1946 

Height: 8.5 m

312

On which river Kabul river falls?

Correct Answer: Indus
Show explanation
Explanation

The Kabul River empties into the Indus River near Attock, Pakistan.

It is the main river in eastern Afghanistan and the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

Native name: د کابل سیند (Da Kābul Sīnd) (Pashto)

It was anciently known as the Cophen

It is a 700-kilometre-long (430 mi) river 

They emerge in the Sanglakh Range of the Hindu Kush mountains in the northeastern part of Maidan Wardak Afghanistan.

Noori a renowned character in Shah Jo Risalo has her grave in:

Correct Answer: Khanjeer Lake

314

River Indus started at.............?

Correct Answer: Tibet
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indus River starts in Tibet

The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

National River of Pakistan: Indus River

6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

Indus River Length: 3,180 km

Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

315

Bolan, Nari, Pishin, Lora Mulla, Hingol, Rakhshan, Dusht and Zhob are the rivers of ____?

Correct Answer: Balochistan
Show explanation
Explanation

These rivers - Bolan, Nari, Pishin, Lora Mulla, Hingol, Rakhshan, Dusht, and Zhob - are all located in the province of Balochistan, Pakistan.

These play a vital role in the region's ecology and economy.

Many of these rivers originate in the mountain ranges of Balochistan and flow into the Arabian Sea or the Indus River.

Hingol River (the longest river in the province) covers a length of 560 kilometres.

316

Which river is not flowing from Jammu And Kahmir

Correct Answer: Ravi
Show explanation

River Indus flow from Country China, India, Pakistan

River Indus flow from State Ladakh, Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, Gilgit-Baltistan, Tibet

Sources of water Tibetan Plateau, Senge Zangbu, Gar River

Tributaries Sutlej River, Chenab River, Jhelum River, Ravi River,

River Sindh Length 3,180 km

Mouth Arabian Sea near Thatta

The Indus River originates in Tibet and follows a circuitous route southward through the Himalaya Mountains to the Arabian Sea,

The river Indus originates from the lake____?

The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

318

Khandhkot gas field is located in :

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

The Kandhkot gas field is located in the Middle Indus Basin of Pakistan.

It was brought onstream in 1987, almost 30 years after its discovery.

This was mainly due to a lack of market and infrastructure required for development of the low calorific gas.

319

The vast alluvial plains lying between the western banks of Manchester lake and Kirthar range is known as

Correct Answer: Indus delta
Show explanation
Explanation

The vast alluvial plains lying between the western banks of Manchester lake and Kirthar range is known as  Indus delta.

320

Father of rivers is called ____________?

Correct Answer: Indus river
Show explanation
Explanation

 The Indus is a transboundary river of Asia and a trans-Himalayan river of South Asia. The 3,180 km river rises in Western Tibet, flows northwest through the Ladakh and Gilgit-Baltistan regions of Kashmir

Indus River Length: 3,180 km

Indus River Mouths: Arabian Sea, Rann of Kutch, Nalsarovar bird sanctuary

Indus River Tributaries: Sutlej River, Chenab River, Jhelum River, Ravi River,

Indus River Sources: Tibetan Plateau, Senge Zangbu, Gar River

Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo۔

Father of rivers: Indus river

321

In Indus basin treaty, on which of the following rivers the right of Pakistan is admitted?

Correct Answer: All
Show explanation
Explanation

The Treaty allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan

while

and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India

**

Indus Waters Treaty, treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan

Indus Water Treaty between India and Pakistan was brokered by World Bank


***


The Treaty was signed at Karachi by Field Marshal Mohammad Ayub Khan,

the then President of Pakistan, Jawaharlal Nehru 

the then Indian Prime Minister, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif 

The Treaty is effective from 1st April 1960 

****

    • 26% of water in the winter season for the Rabi crop of which Pakistan will be deprived by the Baglihar Dam
    • ***
    • Baglihar Dam is built on the Chenab River in the Doda district of Jammu & Kashmir.
    • The hydropower project 'Baglihar Hydroelectric Power Project', is a run-of-the-river power project on the Chenab River.
    • This project was conceived in 1992, approved in 1996 and construction began in 1999

    Tanda Dam is on the river --------------

    Correct Answer: Kohat

    Explanation

    Tanda Dam or Tanda Lake is a small dam

    Tanda Dam is also a lake view park located in Kohat District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa the province of Pakistan

    It became operational on 17 July 1967,

     it was inaugurated by President Ayub Khan in 1962

    Tanda Lake is a protected site under the Ramsar Convention

     It was included as a Ramsar site on July 23, 1976

    354

    Which chain of hills is located in Sindh

    Correct Answer: Kirthar Mountain Range
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Kirthar Mountains are a mountain range that marks the boundary between the Pakistani provinces of Balochistan and Sindh

    Kirthar Mountains, a mountainous region in southern Pakistan.

    It stretches about 300 km south from the Murat River in central-eastern Balochistan to Cape Muari (Monze), west of Karachi in the Arabian Sea.

    The mountains form the boundary between the lower Indus River basin (east) 

    The highest peak of the mountains is Zardak Peak at 7,430 ft (2,260 m).

    Which country's capital is situated near the Margalla Hills?

    Explanation

    Pakistan country capital is present near the  Margalla Hills 

    The Margalla Hills is located north of Islamabad and south of Haripur District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

    The Margalla Hills are part of the Himalayan foothills. 

    The Margalla Hills within the Margalla Hills National Park 

    The Margalla range has an area of 12,605 hectares.

    The highest peak of Margalla Hills is Tilla Charouni

    Elevation: 1,604 m (5,262 ft)

    Which city is called Malika e Kohsar ?

    Correct Answer: Murree
    Show explanation

    It has average altitude of 2,291 metres

    359

    What does Hindukush mean ?

    Correct Answer: Hindu killer
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hindu Kush is generally translated as "Killer of Hindus" or "Hindu-Killer"

    History 

    It is a name linked to the harsh conditions of the mountains where slaves from the Indian subcontinent died during transport.

    The 14th-century traveler Ibn Battuta is often credited with popularizing this explanation.

    In Persian, "Kush" can mean "to kill," and when combined with "Hindu," it forms this interpretation. 


    Additional information


    The Hindu Kush is a mountain range that runs from northeast to southwest, 

    The Hindu Kush range starts near the Pamir Mountains,

    It is an 800-kilometre-long (500 mi) mountain range in Central and South Asia to the west of the Himalayas.

    The highest peak of the Hindu Kush range in Pakistan is Tirich Mir

    Elevation:  (7,708) meters (25,289) 


    360

    Trich Mir mountain is in which mountain range?

    Correct Answer: Hindu kush
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Tirich Mir is the highest mountain of the Hindu Kush range, and the highest mountain in the world outside of the Himalayas–Karakoram range.

    It is located in the Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

    Elevation: 7,708 m

    Mountain range: Hindu Kush


    361

    Daman-e-Koh is located in?

    Correct Answer: Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Daman-e-Koh located in the middle of the Margalla Hills  Islamabad.

    Its name means foot hills.

    It is about 2400 ft from sea level and almost 500 ft from the city of Islamabad.

    Rakaposhi is a mountain in the Gilgat Baltistan region of Pakistan .Rakaposhi means ?

    Correct Answer: Snow covered
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Rakaposhi, is a mountain in the Karakoram mountain range in Pakistan.

    It is situated in the Nagar Valley approximately 100 km north of the city of Gilgit in the Gilgit District of the Gilgit–Baltistan province of Pakistan.

    Rakaposhi means "Snow Covered" in the local language

    363

    Where is Rakaposhi Mountain located?

    Correct Answer: GB
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Rakaposhi  is a mountain in the Karakoram mountain range in the Gilgit-Baltistan territory of Pakistan.

    Its front side is situated in the Bagrote valley, Nagar valley and danyor, approximately 100 km  north of the city of Gilgit.

    364

    What is the Meaning of Nanga Parbat?

    Correct Answer: Naked Mountain
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Kashmiri name Nanga Parbat is derived from the Sanskrit word nagna parvata, meaning “naked mountain.”
    The height of Nanga Parbat at 8,126 m (26,660 ft) above sea level.

    Nanga Parbat, known locally as Diamer

    Worldwide Nanga Parbat is the 9th highest mountain.

    It is also called Killer Mountain Peak.

    in 1954 the mountain peak, of Nanda Parbat was conquered

    First ascent July 3, 1953

    Location Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan

    mountain range is Nanga Parbat is Himalayas, Western Himalaya

    Shehroze Kashif became the youngest Pakistani mountaineer to summit Nanga Parbat

    369

    What is the height of Pothohar Plateau?

    Correct Answer: 300-600 meters
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

    the total area of about 5000 miles2

    located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River

    300-600 meters is the height of the Pothohar Plateau
    ******

    The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the current four districts:

    Jhelum

    Chakwal

    Rawalpindi

    Attock

    ****

    The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

    Potohar Plateau سطح مرتفع پوٹھوہار .consists of:

    District Rawalpindi District
    Islamabad Capital Territory
    Chakwal District
    Jhelum District
    Khushab District,
    Mirpur District
    Islamabad
    Mianwali District,
    Sargodha District (Northern Belt)

    Show explanation

    The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

    the total area of about 5000 miles2

    located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River

    300-600 meters is the height of the Pothohar Plateau

    When is Right to Self-Determination of Kashmir observed_____?

    Correct Answer: 5 January
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Kashmiris observe Right to Self-Determination Day on 5 January.

    On 5 January 1949, the UN Security Council passed a resolution supporting the Kashmiris’ right to decide their future by themselves.

     

    373

    Feroz Khan Noon Airport is the new name of which airport?

    Correct Answer: Gwadar International Airport
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 1 August 2023, National Assembly of Pakistan passed a resolution recommending the government to rename Gwadar International Airport as Feroz Khan Noon saying it will be a tribute to the former Prime Minister.

    The resolution was moved by Rana Qasim Noon.

    Balochi,

    Punjabi 

    Arabic

    Hazrat Sachal Sarmast born 1152 Hijir (1739 CE)

    Hazrat Sachal Sarmast died 1242 Hijri (1827 CE)

    Hazrat Sachal Sarmast shrine Ranipur (Sindh)

    Hazrat Sachal Sarmast known as: a poet of seven languages (شاعر ہفت زبان)

    375

    Sir Radcliffe's award unjustly provided the Indians with land routes too?

    Correct Answer: punjab
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     Sir Radcliffe's award unjustly provided the Indians with land routes to Panjab.

    The Radcliffe Line was the boundary demarcated between the Indian and Pakistani portions of the Punjab Province

    The demarcation line was published on 17 August 1947 upon the Partition of British India.

    376

    The delegation of simla deputation met Lord Minto on

    Correct Answer: 1st October 1906
    Show explanation

    Who was the first Portuguese viceroy of India?

    Correct Answer: Francisco de Almeida
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Francisco de Almeida was the first Portuguese viceroy of India from 1505 to 1509. 
    378

    Which among the following events took place during the viceroyship of Lord Ripon?

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • During the time of Lord Ripon, the age limit for civil service was raised from 18 to 21.
    • First Factory Act was passed in the year 1881.
    • Hunter commission was appointed in the year 1882 for educational reforms.
    379

    Muslims of subcontinent met the viceroy for demanding separate electorates in which year ?

    Correct Answer: 1906
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Simla Deputation was a gathering of 35 prominent Indian Muslim leaders led by the Aga Khan III at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla in October 1906.

    380

    Who is considered as Father of Local Government in the Subcontinent?

    Correct Answer: Lord Rippon
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lord Rippon initiated many reforms to ease the plight of the local Indians during his tenure as the Viceroy. Chief among those reforms was the introduction of local government, the first of its kind in British India. 

    381

    Who was the first Governor General of Bengal___________?

    Correct Answer: Warren Hastings
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Warren Hastings was governor general of Bengal from 1772 to 1785.

    Confused Questions

    First Governor of Bengal:

    Robert Clive

     

    first Governor-General of Bengal was

    Warren Hastings

     

    first Governor-General of India was

    William Bentinck

     

    Who was the first viceroy of India?

    Lord Canning

     

    Who was the last Viceroy of India?

    Lord Mountbatten

    382

    The title of Governor General was changed to Viceroy in the year :

    Correct Answer: 1858 AD
    Show explanation
    Explanation


    Lord Canning was the first viceroy of India

    Lord Mountbatten was the last viceroy of India

    *********

    •Governor-General of Bengal (1773-1833):
    •When East India Company came to India, it controlled Bengal through a post named as "Governor of Bengal"
    •First Governor of Bengal: Robert Clive.
    •After the passing of Regulating Act 1773, the post of Governor
    •of Bengal was converted into "Governor-General of Bengal"
    •first Governor-General of Bengal was Warren Hastings.
    •Governor-General of India (1833-58):Charter Act of 1833, the post name of Governor-General of Bengal again converted into "Governor-General of India"
    •first Governor-General of India was William Bentinck.
    •Viceroy (1858-1947):
    •After the revolt of 1857, the company rule was abolished and India came under the direct control of the British crown.
    •Government of India Act 1858 passed which changed the name of post Governor General of India by Viceroy of India
    •The Viceroy was appointed directly by the British government.

    •The first Viceroy of India was Lord Canning.
    383

    According to which Act, the title of viceroy was to be dropped ?

    Correct Answer: Act of Partition of 1947
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The partition was outlined in the Indian Independence Act 1947 and resulted in the dissolution of the British Raj, i.e. Crown rule in India. 

    ********

    •Governor-General of Bengal (1773-1833):
    •When East India Company came to India, it controlled Bengal through a post named as "Governor of Bengal"
    •First Governor of Bengal: Robert Clive.
    •After the passing of Regulating Act 1773, the post of Governor
    •of Bengal was converted into "Governor-General of Bengal"
    •first Governor-General of Bengal was Warren Hastings.
    •Governor-General of India (1833-58):Charter Act of 1833, the post name of Governor-General of Bengal again converted into "Governor-General of India"
    •first Governor-General of India was William Bentinck.
    •Viceroy (1858-1947):
    •After the revolt of 1857, the company rule was abolished and India came under the direct control of the British crown.
    •Government of India Act 1858 passed which changed the name of post Governor General of India by Viceroy of India
    •The Viceroy was appointed directly by the British government.
    •The first Viceroy of India was Lord Canning.


    384

    Ahmadis were declared Non-Muslims in _______?

    Correct Answer: 1974
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ahmadis are considered non-Muslims by many mainstream Muslims.

    Mirza Ghulam Ahmad founded this movement.

    In 1974, the Pakistani government enacted the 2nd Amendment to officially declare Ahmadis as non-Muslims.

    Pakistan is the only country in the world to do so.

    385

    Uncodified constitution means _______________?

    Correct Answer: Flexible Constitution
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    An uncodified constitution is a type of constitution where the fundamental rules often take the form of customs, usage, precedent and a variety of statutes and legal instruments.

    An understanding of the constitution is obtained through reading commentary by the judiciary, government committees or legal experts.

    386

    Eighteenth Amendment of the Constitution of Pakistan was passed by the National Assembly of Pakistan on ___________ 2010?

    Correct Answer: April 8
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Eighteenth Amendment of the Constitution of Pakistan was passed by the National Assembly of Pakistan on April 8, 2010, removing the power of the President of Pakistan to dissolve the Parliament.

    387

    Half of the Senate of Pakistan members retire after every __________ years and new ones are elected to replace them:

    Correct Answer: Three years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Senate elections are held after 3 years

    The term of the Members of Senate is six years, however, one-half of its members retire after every three years.

    Technocrat/Ulema

    Women

    388

    How many women seats in senate from each province?

    Correct Answer: 4 seats
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    About Senate of Pakistan


    Senate: Upper House of Pakistan

    Senate in Urdu: ایوان بالا

    Formation: 1973

    Original members: 45

    Raised to 63: 1977

    Raised 87: 2 March, 1985

    Raised to 100: 2002

    First Hindu Woman Senator: Kirshna Kumari

    Half of the Senate members retire after: 3 years

    Members term: 6 years

    Head of Senate is called: Chairman

    National Assembly & Senate: Makes up the Parliament of Pakistan

    Seats for each province: 23

    Baluchistan Women Seats: 4

    Sindh Women Seats: 4

    KPK Women Seats: 4

    Punjab Women Seats: 4

    ICT Women Seat: 1

    Total Women Seats: 17

    Senate elections: after 3 years

    First woman deputy chairperson: Dr Noor Jahan Panezai

    Youngest chairman senate: Sadiq Sanjrani

    First chairman Senate: Habib Ullah Khan




    389

    How many general seats Balochistan has in the National Assembly?

    Correct Answer: 16
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    General Seats in National Assembly

    Punjab: 141

    Sindh: 61

    Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: 45

    Balochistan: 16

    Islamabad: 3

    Total general seats in NA: 266

    390

    President officer of the National assembly of Pakistan is called----.

    Correct Answer: speaker
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • President officer of the National assembly of Pakistan is called the speaker
    • **
    • The Speaker of the House is the presiding officer of the National Assembly. The speaker is assisted by the Deputy Speaker.
    • **
    • The first Speaker/President of the National Assembly of Pakistan, Muhammad Ali Jinnah
    • The current Speaker and Deputy Speaker are Raja Pervaiz Ashraf (PPP) and, Zahid Akram Durrani (MMA) respectively.
    391

    MNA stands for _______?

    Correct Answer: Member of National Assembly
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     In Pakistan, MNA stands for Member of the National Assembly.

    Total 336 MNA of NA


    Details:
    Function: Elected representative in the lower house of Pakistan’s bicameral legislature (Parliament).
    Term Duration: 5 years, unless the Assembly is dissolved earlier.
    Election: MNAs are elected through general elections, which are typically held every five years.

    392

    In Pakistan, the Prime Minister and cabinet members are chosen from:

    Correct Answer: Parliament
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    the Prime Minister and cabinet members are chosen from parliament. 

    Prime Minister cannot appoint more than 5 advisers

    According to 18th amendment (Article 92(1).) Total strength of the cabinet does not exceed 11% of the total parliament members (Including Senate & National Assembly)

     AGM 24-12-2022


    393

    How many general seats Punjab has in the National Assembly?

    Correct Answer: 141
    Show explanation

    The Provincial Assembly of Punjab will have a total of 371 seats:

    297 general seats,

    66 reserved for women,

    8 reserved for non-Muslims.

    General Seats in National Assembly

    Punjab: 141

    Sindh: 61

    Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: 45

    Balochistan: 16

    Islamabad: 3

    Total general seats in NA: 266

    Sindh: 61

    395

    What is the minimum age of becoming the candidate for National Assembly of Pakistan

    Correct Answer: 25 years
    Show explanation
