    Minimum age for President of Pakistan: 45 Years

    Minimum age for Governor of Pakistan: 35 Years

    Minimum age for PM of Pakistan: 35 Years

    Minimum age for Senator of Pakistan: 30 Years

    Minimum age for National Assembly Member: 25 Years

    396

    Who was the first president of 1st constituent Assembly?

    Correct Answer: Quaid e Azam
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Moulvi Tamiz ud Din  appointed  as dupty  president of the constituent Assembly 

    397

    Which Muslim leadernch of the Muslim League______?

    Correct Answer: Syed Ameer Ali
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Syed Ameer Ali established the London Muslim League in 1908.

    The London Muslim League was an independent body and not a branch of All India Muslim League.

    Syed Ameer Ali Died on 3 August 1928 United Kingdom

    398

    Which is the outcome of the first joint meeting of Indian National Congress and All India Muslim League___________?

    Correct Answer: Lucknow pact
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Lucknow Pact was an agreement held in Lucknow in December 1916.

    Demands presented to the British

    1.    All provinces should be granted autonomy.

    2.    Separating the executive from the judiciary

    3.    The number of elected seats should be increased.

    4.    Laws which were passed by large majorities in the councils should be accepted

    5.    Minorities in the provinces should be protected.

    399

    In which city first annual meeting of All India Muslim League was held?

    Correct Answer: Karachi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The first annual session of the Muslim League was held in Karachi in 1907.

    The Muslim League was formed in 1906 in Dhaka,

    From 27th December to 30 December 1906.

    All India Muslim League Founder: Nawab Khwaja Salimullah

    All India Muslim League 1st President: Sir Agha Khan

    All India Muslim League Founded: 30 December 1906 at Dacca, British India (now Dhaka, Bangladesh)

    All India Muslim League Headquarters: Lucknow

    All India Muslim League Dissolved: 14 August 1947

    جب 1947 میں مسلم لیگ ختم ہوئی تو یہ تین پارٹیاں بن گئی

    1- Muslim League in Pakistan

    2- Awami League in Bangladesh

    3- Indian Union Muslim League in India

    400

    When the Indian Muslim League was inducted into the interim government in 1946. Liaqat Ali Khan was assigned the portfolio of:

    Correct Answer: Finance
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • finance
    • whilst the government requested the Muslim League to send 5 nominees for representation within the interim government
    • Khan turned into requested to lead the League institution inside the cupboard. He turned into given the portfolio of finance.
    401

    Name the Sindhi leader who supported Pakistan Resolution from the province of Sindh:

    Correct Answer: G.M. Syed
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    G. M. Syed, an influential Sindhi activist, revolutionary and Sufi and later one of the important leaders in the forefront of the Sindh independence movement, joined the Muslim League in 1938 and presented the Pakistan resolution in the Sindh Assembly.

    Signatories: All-India Muslim League

    Presented: 22 March 1940

    Location: Lahore

    402

    In which year the Congress and the Muslim League held a joint session for the first time at Lucknow?

    Correct Answer: 1916
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Correct answer C

    1916

    The Lucknow Pact was an agreement  between the Indian National Congress and the Muslim Federation (AIML) at a joint meeting of  the two parties  in Lucknow in December 1916. The Lucknow Pact was seen as a beacon of hope for the unity of Hindus and Muslims. This is the first time  Hindus and Muslims have jointly called for political reform in Britain. This has led to a growing belief in British India that home rules are a real possibility.


    403

    Malik Barkat Ali and Ghazanfar Ali were elected as Muslim League Candidates in election of:

    Correct Answer: 1937
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In the 1937 elections, only two Muslim Leaguers — Malik Barkat Ali and Raja Ghazanfar — were elected,

    but Raja defected to the Unionist Party and for eight years,

    Malik Barkat Ali alone represented the League in Punjab’s legislature.

    Malik Barkat Ali was an Indian Muslim politician, lawyer, and journalist.

    Party: All-India Muslim League

    ND06-08-2023

    Show explanation

    • *
    • Simon Commission was appointed in 1928

    • It comprises 7 members of the British

    • It was opposed by both Jinnah & Gandhi

    Quaid-e-Azam returned to India to organize Muslim League in

    Correct Answer: 1934
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Quaid e Azam join All-India Muslim League in 1913 till 1947

    Quaid e Azam join Pakistan Muslim League in 1947 till 1948)

    Quaid e Azam joined the All India Muslim League at the persuasion of Molana Muhammad Ali Johar.

    All India Muslim League Founder Nawab Khwaja Salimullah

    ***

    ND16-1-2023

    408

    One of the mean object of all India Muslim League at the time of its creation was

    Correct Answer: To be loyal to the Indian British Government
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The communal idea that the Muslims are a separate nation was sown into the Indian political ethos first by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.

    On 30 December 1906, around 3000 delegates attended a conference of the Muhammadan Educational Conference at Dhaka in which the ban on politics was removed and a motion was moved to form the AIML.

    The name was proposed by Nawab Khwaja Sir Salimullah Bahadur and seconded by Hakim Ajmal Khan.

    The AIML was the first Muslim political party of India.

    The founders of the Muslim League were: Khwaja Salimullah, Vikar-ul-Mulk, Syed Amir Ali, Syed Nabiullah, Khan Bahadur Ghulam and Mustafa Chowdhury.

    The first Honorary President of the League was Sir Sultan Muhammad Shah (Aga Khan III).

    The objectives of the league were:

    1. To create among Muslims the feelings of loyalty towards the British Government.
    2. To safeguard the political rights of the Muslims and to convey the same to the government.
    3. To prevent the rise of prejudice against other communities of India among the Muslims.

    Muhammad Ali Jinnah joined the league in 1913.

    409

    Where was the session of Muslim League held when the resolution demanding the creation of Pakistan was passed?

    Correct Answer: Lahore
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The All India Muslim League met in Lahore in March 1940 and adopted a resolution that came to be known as the Lahore Resolution or Pakistan Resolution. Its the first hand demand was partition of the country and creation of Muslim nation named Pakistan.

    410

    In Pakistan _______% of the population lives below the national poverty line (2017)?

    Correct Answer: 24.3%
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to a report submitted by Ministry of Planning and Development in the National Assembly of Pakistan, about 24.3% Pakistani lived below the poverty line which translates into 55 million people

    411

    According to PBS, inflation rises to ________ percent in September from 8.2 percent in August 2020.

    Correct Answer: 9 percent
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to Pakistan Bureau of Statistics (PBS), inflation rises to 9 percent in September from 8.2 percent in August.

    This data was released on October 7, 2020.

    412

    When Second Census was held in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 1961
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.

    Total Population of west Pakistan was 42.9 Million

    Total Males 22.96 Million

    Total Females 19.92 Million

    West Pakistan had a literacy rate of 16.9%

    The population growth rate of Pakistan in 1961 was 2.4%.


    **********

    Total East and west Pakistan population was 93 million

    Literacy was 19.2%

    East Pakistan had a literacy rate of 21.5%

    The population growth rate in 1961 was 2.4%.


    413

    When Third Census was held in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 1972
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.

    Total Population of Pakistan in 1972 was 65.3 Million

    Total Males 34.83 Million

    Total Females 30.48 Million

    Growth rates 3.5% per annum

    Literacy was 21.70%

    414

    When Fifth Census was held in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 1998
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.

    Total Population of Pakistan in 1998 was 132.4 Million

    Total Males  68.87 Million

    Total Females 63.48 Million

    Growth rates 2.6% per annum

    Literacy rate for males was 54.8%

    Literacy rate for females was 43.2%

    **************

    First Census was held in Pakistan in 1951.

    The second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.

    The third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.

    Fourth Census was held in Pakistan in 1981.

    The fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.

    Sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.

    Seventh in 2023

    First census in the subcontinent occurred during the reign of which viceroy?

    Correct Answer: Lord Mayo
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Census has been undertaken every 10 years beginning in 1872 under British viceroy Lord Mayo

    the first complete census was taken in the sub-continent in 1881


    while

    The first census in Indo Pak subcontinent was held in

    After independence, the first census of Pakistan was conducted in 1951

    the second in 1961 

    the third census was held in 1972 instead of 1971

    On which date is National Voter's Day celebrated in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 7 December

    National Voter's Day is celebrated in Pakistan on 7th December annually.

    The day is marked in commemoration of the first general elections of Pakistan held on December 7, 1970.

    At least __________ votes are required to be Chief Minister of Punjab.

    Correct Answer: 186
    Show explanation

    Required votes for CM in Punjab: 186

    420

    When the first adult based elections held in Pakistan

    Correct Answer: 1970
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The first direct elections in the country after independence took place for the  Punjab State Legislature between 10 and 20 March 1951.

    The election was held with 197 seats.

    The remaining seats were unanimously filled, but more than 939 candidates ran for 189 seats.

    421

    How many woman candidates made it to the National Assembly through the direct General Elections 2018?

    Correct Answer: 10
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The parliament initially had 210 seats

    Including 10 women, but in 1985 it increased to 237 seats, and then to 342 seats.

    Currently, there are  342 seats in the Diet.

    Of these, 272 are directly filled with elections.

    Correct Answer: M1 and M2
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    M1 and M2 motorways are ready to be used as runways in any emergency.

    All motorways in Pakistan are prefixed with the letter 'M' (for "Motorway")

     There are a total of 16 motorways

    There are a total of 11 of which are operational,

    while

    some are under construction and others are planned.

    Who was the first elected Prime Minister of Pakistan

    Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    General elections were held in Pakistan on 7 December 1970.

    They were the first general elections since the independence of Pakistan.


    430

    President has all the executives, legislative, judicial and emergency powers in Pakistan.But he exercise them with the advice of _________?

    Correct Answer: Both
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Arif Alvi was sworn in as the 13th President of Islamic Republic of Pakistan on 9th September 2018.

    431

    If PM of Pakistan wants a bill to be passed.It is necessary to get bill approved from ______?

    Correct Answer: All of the above
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    432

    Who works as acting PM of Pakistan in the absence of PM?

    Correct Answer: Speaker national assembly
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    During the President's absence, the chairman senate is empowered with the duties of the presidency; in rare events involving the absence of the chairman, the presidential duties are usually held by Speaker National Assembly.
    433

    In what condition the opposition may declare a vote of no confidence against PM in national assembly of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: All of the above
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Currently the National Assembly can not be dissolved by the President of Pakistan; it is dissolved by the Prime Minister of Pakistan.
    434

    The basic duty of every citizen is :

    Correct Answer: Obedience to constitution and law
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 8-28 of 1973 constitution of Pakistan  deals  with  fundamental rights 

    435

    Fundamental Rights and Principles of Policy are included in which part of the constitution of Pakistan, 1973

    Correct Answer: Part II
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Answer: 

    A

    In this part, unless otherwise specified in the context, Fundamental Rights and Principles of Policy are included 

    436

    According to the constitution of Pakistan the education shall be free and compulsory to all citizens of age

    Correct Answer: 5-16 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 8-28 of 1973 constitution of Pakistan  deals  with  fundamental rights 

    437

    National Assembly is bound to convene the assembly session within ________ days after submission of a no confidence vote.

    Correct Answer: 14
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 08-03-2022, the opposition, submitted a resolution of no-confidence against Prime Minister Imran Khan. Under the Constitution, National Assembly Speaker Asad Qaiser is bound to convene the assembly session within 14 days after submission of the requisition notice.

    438

    How many members of National Assembly are required to present a no confidence against a prime minister?

    Correct Answer: At least 20 percent
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Under the Constitution, 68 members (20 per cent of the total members) of the National Assembly can submit a no-confidence resolution against the prime minister.

    439

    Article 219 of the Constitution of Pakistan deals with ___________

    Correct Answer: Function and duties of the election commission
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 219: Duties of Commission

    The Commission shall be charged with the duty of,

    a. preparing electoral rolls for election to the National Assembly and the Provincial Assemblies, and revising such rolls annually;

    b. organizing and conducting election to the Senate or to fill casual vacancies in a House or a Provincial Assembly; and

    c. appointing Election Tribunals;

    d. the holding of general elections to the National Assembly, Provincial Assemblies and the local governments; and

    e. such other functions as may be specified by an Act of Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).

    440

    Article 5 of the constitution of Pakistan is about:

    Correct Answer: Loyalty to the State is the basic duty of every citizen
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to Article 5 of Pakistan

    Loyalty to the State is the basic duty of every citizen and obedience to the Constitution and law is the [inviolable] obligation of every citizen wherever he may be and of every other person for the time being within Pakistan.

     

    441

    According to constitution, Pakistani federal cabinet including ministers and ministers of state cannot exceed _____ members.

    Correct Answer: 49
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Prime Minister cannot appoint more than 5 advisers

    According to 18th amendment (Article 92(1).) Total strength of the cabinet does not exceed 11% of the total parliament members (Including Senate & National Assembly)

     

    442

    The Council of Common Interests (CCI) is worked under which Ministry?

    Correct Answer: Ministry of Inter Provincial Coordination
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Council of Common Interests (CCI) is a constitutional body established under Article 153 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973.

    443

    Which article of the constitution deals with 'Vote of No Confidence' against Prime Minister of Pakistan__________?

    Correct Answer: article 95
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 95

    1. A vote of no-confidence moved by not less than twenty percent of the total

     membership of the National Assembly

    2. A resolution referred to in clause (1) shall not be voted upon before the expiration of three days, or later than seven days, from the day on which such resolution is moved in the National Assembly.

    3. A resolution referred to in clause (1) shall not be moved in the National Assembly while the National Assembly is considering demands for grants submitted to it in the Annual Budget Statement.

    4. If the resolution referred to in clause (1) is passed by a majority of the total membership of the National Assembly, the Prime Minister shall cease to hold office.

    444

    How many signatures required to table a no-confidence motion against Chief Minister Balocshitan___?

    Correct Answer: 20%
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to the Constitution, a no-confidence motion needs the signatures of 20 per cent of the make-up of the legislative body to be tabled for discussion, while half the members must vote in favour of it to be passed.

    445

    Which Article of the Pakistani constitution 1973 states “The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of five to sixteen years in such manner as may be determined by law?

    Correct Answer: 25-A
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In 2010, Article 25-A of the Pakistani Constitution was created, stating that "The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of five to sixteen years in such manner as may be determined by law."

    446

    According to the fundamental rights no child below the age of ___shall be engaged in any factory or mine or any other hazardous employment

    Correct Answer: 14 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 8-28 of 1973 constitution of Pakistan  deals  with  fundamental rights 

    447

    Who is the Head of the Cabinet?

    Correct Answer: PM
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Prime minister is the leader of the cabinet
    • ***
    • The Cabinet of Pakistan is a formal body composed of senior government officials chosen and led by the Prime Minister.
    • Shehbaz Sharif is the current PM of Pakistan.
    • ***
    • Lord Pethick Lawrence  headed the Cabinet Mission
    • The first Cabinet of Pakistan consisted of 7 members
    • ***
    • there are the following member including in the first cabinet

    1-      Liaquat Ali Khan

    Prime Minister, Foreign Minister, Defence Minister, and Minister for Common Wealth Relations.

    2-      Malik Ghulam Mohammad

    Finance Minister.

    3-      Sardar Abdul Rab Nishtar

    Transport and Communication Minister.

    4-      Raja Ghazanfar Ali Khan

    Minister for Food, Agriculture, and Health.

    5-      Jugindarnath Mandal

    Minister for Law and Labour.

    6-      Fazal-ur-Rehman

    Interior Minister and Minister for Information and Education.

    7-      Ibrahim Ismail Chandigarh

    Minister for Trade, Industry, and Construction.


    448

    In which constitution the restriction of a Muslim was not for the Prime Minister

    Correct Answer: 1973
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In the 1973 constitution, the restriction of a Muslim was not for the Prime Minister
    • ***
    • the definition of a Muslim in the 1973 Constitution:
    • The Constitution put stipulations on the eligibility of becoming President and Prime Minister that only "Muslim" of not less than forty-five years of age is qualified for becoming the Prime Minister.
    • No law repugnant to Islam shall be enacted and the present laws shall also be Islamised.


    449

    The present constitution of Pakistan was adopted in:

    Correct Answer: 1973
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The present constitution of Pakistan was adopted on 14 August 1973 by ZA Bhatto


    اس ٹاپک کے متعلق انتہائی اہم سوالات پڑھیں

    Constitution 1973




    450

    The constitution is the state's -----Law.

    Correct Answer: Fundamental
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Constitutional law deals with the fundamental principles by which the government exercises its authority.
    • the Constitution is a law Written in 1787, ratified in 1788, and in operation since 1789
    • the United States Constitution is the world's longest-surviving written charter of government.
    • The Constitution was interpreted, in 1819, as giving the Supreme Court
    • The Constitution sets forth the three principal branches of the federal government and outlines their jurisdictions.
    • Constitutional laws can be both written and unwritten

    ****

    ND29-11-2022

    451

    In present constitution, how many lists of power are given for the division of powers between Provinces and Federation?

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    1. Federal List

    2. Provincial List

    3. Concurrent List 

    452

    Upper part of Parliament of Pakistan is called:

    Correct Answer: Senate
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Senate members are 100 till the 2023 election.

    The National Assembly of the parliament consists of 336 members old was 342 reducing KPK from 61 to 55.

    It becomes 342 after the 26th amendment

    National Assembly is the lower  house of parliament 

    Senate is the Upper house of parliament 


    NamePunjabSindhKPKBaluchistanICTTotal
    General1416145163266
    Women3214104-60
    Non-Muslims-----10
    Total1737555203336

    According to Article 51 (1) and 3(a) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan

    Article 51. (1) There shall be [three hundred and thirty-six] seats for members in the National Assembly, including seats reserved for women and non-Muslims.

    *****

    RY 10-01-2023

    Reference Click

    453

    Which Article of Constitution gives Supreme Court the Suo Motu powers?

    Correct Answer: Article 184(3)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Suo motu is a latin phrase which means “on its own”.

    This means that the court has taken the matter into cognisance on its own and without any plaintiff

    ******

    RY 15-01-2023


    454

    All bills passed by the parliament of Pakistan are not approved until signed by _________?

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    All bills passed by the parliament of Pakistan are not approved until signed by President.

    455

    Urdu was made national language of Pakistan in which Constitution :

    Correct Answer: 1973
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The 1973 Constitution recognizes Urdu as Pakistan's only national language.

    Article 251 of the Constitution says “arrangements shall be made for  being used for official and other purposes within next fifteen years”

    ***

    RY5022023

    456

    Under which article of constitution Special Court announced death sentence of (Rtd) General Pervaiz Musharraf in 2019_?

    Correct Answer: Article 6
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 6 means high treason (سنگین غداری)



    Know everything about Musharraf

    Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

    Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

    Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

    Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

    Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

    Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

    In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

    In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

    In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

    Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

    Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

    Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

    Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

    In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

    He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

    On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

    Died: 5 February 2023

    Dying place: Dubai, UAE

    Disease: amyloidosis

    Aged: 79

    Buried: Karachi

     

     

    457

    Urdu was declared the national language of Pakistan in which year?

    Correct Answer: April 1954
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Confusion

    Urdu is the national language of Pakistan since 14 August 1947.

    In 1954 Urdu and Bengali were declared the national language of Pakistan.

    The 1973 Constitution recognizes Urdu as Pakistan’s only national language. 

    ****

    Urdu declared as official language In subcontinent in 1832

    458

    Under which article, Chief Justice of Pakistan (CJP) take Suo-Muto notice?

    Correct Answer: 184(3)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    آئین کا آرٹیکل 184(3) سپریم کورٹ کے اصل دائرہ اختیار کا تعین کرتا ہے

    اور اسے پاکستان کے شہریوں کے "بنیادی حقوق میں سے کسی ایک کے نفاذ" کے حوالے سے "عوامی اہمیت" کے سوال سے متعلق معاملات میں دائرہ اختیار سنبھالنے کے قابل بناتا ہے۔

    459

    After abrogation of first constitution, the new constitution was enforced by Ayoub Khan on:

    Correct Answer: June 1962
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Iskandar Ali Mirza, the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

    There were 234 articles 13 parts and 6 schedules.


    Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation.

    He enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962.

    ND31-12-2022

    460

    Which constitution of Pakistan is called Unicameral?

    Correct Answer: 1962
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Under the 1956 Constitution, Parliament was unicameral.

    Legislative powers vested in the Parliament, which consisted of the President and the National Assembly comprising 300 Members divided between East Pakistan and West Pakistan

    ****

    Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

    while

    Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

                All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

                1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

                ***

                Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

                ***

                In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

                ****

                40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

                **

                The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

                **

                The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

                **

                The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.



                461

                The first constituent Assembly met under the Chairmanship of

                Correct Answer: Jugandara Nath Mandal
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                first constituent Assembly met under the Chairmanship of Jugandara  Nath  Mandal On 10 Aug  1947

                462

                Who was the only Muslim member in the house who opposed the Objectives Resolution 1949 in the Assembly?

                Correct Answer: Main Iftikar-ud-Din
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Mian Muhammad Iftikharuddin was the only Muslim member who opposed the resolution.

                ****

                The Objectives Resolution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan on March 12, 1949.

                Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan, had presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949.

                Out of 75 members of the assembly, 21 voted for opposing it. 

                All the amendments proposed by minority members were rejected.

                Consequently, all ten of them voted against it.

                ****

                Mian Muhammad Iftikharuddin was the only Muslim member in the house who opposed the resolution.

                To him the resolution was vague and many words used in it do not mean anything.

                He further suggested that such a resolution should not only be the product of Muslim League members sitting in the assembly alone.

                Rather it was supposed to be the voice of seventy million people of Pakistan.

                RK151222

                463

                Which country became the first associate CERN member from Asia on 19th December 2014?

                Correct Answer: Pakistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                CERN is European Organization for Nuclear Research.
                CERN Established in                 1954
                CERN Headquarters in                  Geneva, Switzerland
                CERN Membership                  23 countries
                464

                Status of Pakistan relating to Shanghai Cooperation Organisation is:

                Correct Answer: Full Member
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • On 9 June, 2017 India and Pakistan became full members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO).
                • The SCO Headquarters: Beijing, China
                • The SCO Founded: June 15, 2001
                • The SCO currently comprises of nine Members States (China, India, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, Pakistan, Tajikistan, Iran and Uzbekistan), four Observer States

                • *****

                  RY4-12-2022



                465

                List of International organizations Joined by Pakistan

                Correct Answer: check in details
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                International organizations Joined by Pakistan

                1. United Nations in 1947.

                2. Asian Development Bank (ADB)

                3. Association of Southeast Asian Nations Regional Forum (ARF) Join 2nd July, 2004.

                4. Colombo Plan in 1950

                5. Commonwealth of Nations. Joined in 1947 (left in 1972 & rejoined in 1986)

                6. Central Treat Organization (CENTO), known as the Baghdad Pact, was adopted in 1955 by Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, Turkey and the United Kingdom. It was dissolved in 1979.

                7. Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO) established in 1985 by Iran, Pakistan & Turkey.

                8. Food & Agriculture Organization (FAO) on September 7, 1947.

                9. Group of 24 in 1971

                10. Group of 77 (1976-1977, 1992-2009)

                11. International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) on May 2nd 1957.

                12. International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) in 1952

                13. International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) in 1955.

                14. International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) on November 25, 1947.

                15. International Criminal Court (ICCt)

                16. International Criminal Police Organization (Interpol) in May, 1952.

                17. International Development Association (IDA) in 1962.

                18.International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRCS)

                19. International Finance Corporation (IFA) in 1955.

                20. International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) in 1978.

                21. International Hydrographic Organization (IHO)

                22. International Labour Organization (ILO) in 1947.

                23. International Maritime Organization (IMO) in 1958.

                24. International Mobile Satellite Organization (IMSO) on February 6,1985.

                25. International Monetary Fund (IMF) in July 1950.

                26. International Olympic Committee (IOC) in 1948.

                27. International Organization for Migration (IOM) in November 1992.

                28. International Organization of Standardization (ISO)

                29. International Telecom Union (ITU) on 26th August 1947.

                30. International Telecommunication Satellite Organization (ITSO)

                31. International Trade Union Confederation (ITUC)

                32. Inter Parliamentary Union (IPU) in 1948.

                33. Islamic Development Bank (IDB

                34. Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA) in 1991

                35. Nonaligned Movement (NAM) in 1977.

                36. Organization of Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) in 1993.

                37. Organization of American States (OAS) Pakistan is an observer. In January 6, 1988.

                38. Organization of Islamic Conference (OIC. Pakistan joined in 1969.

                39. Partnership for Peace (PFP)

                40. Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) Pakistan joined on 9 June 2017.

                41. South Asia Co-operative Environment Program (SACEP) in 1982.

                42. Southeast Asia Treaty Organization (SEATO). SEATO was dissolved on June 30, 1977

                43. South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) Bhutan, Maldives, Pakistan, India, and Nepal & Sri Lanka. In April 2007.

                44. African Union/ United Nation Hybrid Operation in Darfur (UNAMID)

                45. United Nation Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)

                46.United Nations Education, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) on 14th September 1949.

                47. United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) in 1976.

                48. United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) in 1968.

                49. United Nations Integrated Mission in the Sudan (UNIMS)

                50. United Nations Observer Mission in Georgia (UNOMIG) on 23rd July 1995

                51. United Nations Operation in Cote d’vloire (UNOCI)

                52. United Nations Organization Mission in Democratic Republic of the Congo (MONUC)

                53. Universal Postal Union (UPU) on 10-11-1947.

                54. World Confederation of Labour (WCL)

                55. World Customs Organization (WCO)

                56. World Federation of Trade Unions (WFTU)

                57. World Health Organization (WHO)

                58. World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) in 1977.

                59. World Metrological Organization (WMO)

                60. World Tourism Organization (UNWTO)

                61. World Trade Organization (WTO) in 1995

                How many times Pakistan has been elected to the United Nation’s body on Human Rights Council (HRC)?

                Georgia: a country in Asia and Europe

                 

                469

                Which of the following only country did not pull out from SAARC 19th Conference scheduled in November 2016 in Islamabad (Pak)?

                Correct Answer: Nepal
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation is an economic and political organization of eight countries in South Asia. 

                It was established in 1985.

                470

                Which country recently offered gas to Pakistan

                Correct Answer: Turkmenistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                • The Turkmenistan-Afghanistan-Pakistan-India (TAPI) pipeline, also known as the Trans-Afghanistan pipeline, is a natural gas pipe developed by  Galkynysh – TAPI Pipeline Company Limited with the participation of the Asian Development Bank.
                • It is a line.  The pipeline transports natural gas from the Garkinish gas field in Turkmenistan via Afghanistan to Pakistan and then to India. 
                • Construction of the project will begin in Turkmenistan on  December 13, 2015, 
                • work on the Afghanistan section will begin in February 2018, and work on the Pakistan section will begin in December 2018.
                471

                Pakistan became a member of International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in_________?

                Correct Answer: 1957
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The International Atomic Energy Agency is an international organization that seeks to promote the peaceful use of nuclear energy.

                Inhibit its use for any military purpose, including nuclear weapons. 

                Headquarters: Vienna, Austria

                Founded: July 29, 1957

                472

                Which military alliance had Pakistan as its member?

                Correct Answer: SEATO
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                In 1954, Pakistan became a member of SEATO along with the United States, Britain, France, Thailand, the Philippines, Australia and New Zealand.

                473

                Pakistan is not a member of which of the following?

                Correct Answer: NPT
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                NPT stands for:  Non-Proliferation Treaty

                NPT signed: 1968

                Note: India, Israel, North Korea, Pakistan and South Sudan are not members of NPT

                 

                474

                Pakistan joined SCO as a full member at a summit 9 June 2017 in?

                Correct Answer: Astana
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                On 9 June 2017, at a summit in Astana, India and Pakistan officially joined SCO as full members.

                • On 9 June, 2017 India and Pakistan became full members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO).
                • The SCO Headquarters: Beijing, China
                • The SCO Founded: June 15, 2001
                • The SCO currently comprises of nine Members States (China, India, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, Pakistan, Tajikistan, Iran and Uzbekistan), four Observer States

                • On 9 July 2023, Iran became 9th member of SCO. 

                475

                In July 2008, Pakistan signed the agreement of Extradition of criminals with which country?

                Correct Answer: Iran
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                On 23 July 2008, an agreement signed between Iran and Pakistan which provides extradition of criminals after meeting legal requirements.

                 

                476

                Where was the second OIC Summit Conference held?

                Correct Answer: Lahore
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The second OIC Summit Conference held from February 22, 1974 to February 24, 1974 in Lahore, Pakistan.

                The Organization was established upon a decision of the historical summit which took place in Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco on 12th Rajab 1389 Hijra (25 September 1969)

                ****

                THE 14TH ISLAMIC SUMMIT CONFERENCE Makkah, Saudi Arabia (31 May 2019)

                The 13th Islamic Summit Conference (Unity and Solidarity For Justice and Peace)  Istanbul, Republic of Turkey (14-15 April 2016)

                12th Islamic Summit Conference (The Muslim World: New Challenges & Expanding Opportunities) Cairo, Egypt (6-7 February 2013)

                11th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference (Session of The Muslim Ummah in The 21St Century) Dakar, Senegal (13-14 March 2008)

                10th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Putrajaya, Malaysia (16-17 October 2003)

                9th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Doha, State of Qatar (12-13 November 2000)

                8th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Tehran, Islamic Republic of Iran (9-11 December 1997)

                7th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (13-15 December 1994)

                6th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Dakar, Republic of Senegal (9 - 11 December 1991)

                5th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Kuwait, State of Kuwait (26-29 January 1987)

                4th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (16-19 January 1984)

                3rd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Makkah Al-Mukarramah, Saudi Arabia (25-28 January 1981)

                2nd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Lahore,  Islamic Republic of Pakistan (22 - 24 February 1974)

                1st Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco (September 1969)

                ND04-08-2023


                477

                One belt one road (OBOR) is a project of China which is?

                Correct Answer: Mainly to connect China with Far East Asia
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                ·         As of January 2019, the list totals 790 projects

                ·         OBOR stands for One Belt One Road

                 OBOR was Initially announced in the year 2013

                ·         What Is One Belt One Road (OBOR)?

                ·         OBOR, the brainchild of Chinese President Xi Jinping, is an ambitious economic development and commercial project spread across the continents of Asia, Africa, and Europe.

                ·         Dubbed the “Project of the Century” by the Chinese authorities, OBOR spans about 78 countries  

                       SH1-8-2023

                Correct Answer: 1947

                Name the educational institution founded by Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum in KPK?

                He founded the college in 1913, inspired by the visionary educational principles of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan

                Roshan Khan was a famous player of:

                493

                Pakistani teenager Ahmed Raza will be conducting the coin toss in the game between _____________ and Costa Rica in their FIFA World Cup match on 23-06-2018?

                Correct Answer: Brazil
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 2018 FIFA World Cup was an international football tournament contested by men's national teams and took place between 14 June and 15 July 2018 in Russia.

                It was the 21st FIFA World Cup, a worldwide football tournament held once every four years.

                It was the eleventh time the championships had been held in Europe, and the first time they were held in Eastern Europe.

                At an estimated cost of over $14.2 billion, it was the most expensive World Cup to date

                494

                Who has made most number of runs in ODI cricket for Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Inzamam-ul-Haq
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Inzamam-ul-Haq is the only Pakistani batsman to score 20,000 runs in international cricket.

                Matches (Mat): 375

                Innings (Inn): 348

                Runs: 11,701

                Highest Score: 137*

                Batting Average (Avg): 39.53

                Centuries (100s): 10

                Fifties (50s): 83

                VU CBT FIA PAPER 25-12-2021

                495

                In world cup 1992 which bowler taken the last wicket ?

                Correct Answer: Imran Khan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 1992 Cricket World Cup was the fifth staging of the Cricket World Cup.

                Pakistan beating England by 22 runs in the final to become the World Cup champions for the first time. 

                Dates: Feb 22, 1992 – Mar 25, 1992

                Champions: Pakistan (1st title)

                Matches: 39

                Most runs: Martin Crowe (456)

                Most wickets: Wasim Akram (18)

                Participants: 9

                Location: Australia, New Zealand

                496

                Pakistan played first International Hockey matches

                Correct Answer: 1948
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                On 2 August 1948, Pakistan national team, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara, officially went on to play their first international game and tournament against Belgium winning the game 2–0 at the 1948 London Olympics.

                Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

                 Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

                1971

                1978

                1982

                1994.

                ***

                Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

                Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.


                Pakistan played its first International Hockey matches in 1948

                In 1994 year, Pakistan was the world champion of Hockey

                In 1994 en Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time

                ***


                Muhammad Yousaf was born in 1952

                Mohammed Yousuf is a Pakistani snooker player.

                He is the winner of the 1994 IBSF World Snooker Championship,

                2006 IBSF World Masters Championship,

                and 1998 ACBS Asian Snooker Championship.
